close_reason: CLAUDE.md 'Monorepo Dependency Management → Python' rewritten to reflect uv workspace pattern. New docs/user-manual/src/workflows/python-workspace.md covers the turnkey.* namespace convention, hatchling config, cross-member deps, non-packaged members, the dual uv/Buck2 track, and how downstream monorepos pick their own namespace. Linked from SUMMARY.md and cross-referenced from the existing Python language page.
description:
Two docs touched:

1. Update CLAUDE.md 'Monorepo Dependency Management → Python' section:
   - Replace 'All Python code uses the root pyproject.toml. No nested pyproject files.' with the uv workspace pattern.
   - Note: single uv.lock is the single-resolution-point; per-member pyprojects own their declarations (parallel to Cargo's [workspace.dependencies] is approximate, not exact — call this out).
   - Mention the turnkey.<name> namespace convention.

2. Create docs/user-manual/src/workflows/python-workspace.md covering:
   - Why uv workspaces (explicit parallel to Cargo workspaces)
   - The turnkey.<name> namespace convention; downstream monorepos pick their own (e.g., acme.<name>)
   - hatchling force-include pattern for source remap (members don't physically restructure)
   - How uv export → pydeps-gen → pydeps cell composes with the Buck2 track
   - Cross-member deps via [tool.uv.sources] { workspace = true }
   - Add link from docs/user-manual/src/SUMMARY.md
title: Document Python workspace convention for downstream consumers
